class_schedule.topic: Email Marketing Strategy and the Subscriber Lifecycle
learning_objectives: Map the email subscriber lifecycle from acquisition through retention and reactivation
Design an email marketing strategy aligned with business objectives and audience needs
Evaluate the role of email in the broader digital marketing ecosystem

lecture_notes.introduction: Email remains the highest-ROI digital marketing channel with an average return of $36-42 for every $1 spent. This course teaches you to build, segment, automate, and optimize email programs that drive measurable business results while maintaining subscriber trust.
key_concepts: Email ROI: consistently the highest-returning digital channel, surpassing social and paid search
Subscriber lifecycle: acquisition, welcome, engagement, conversion, retention, reactivation, sunset
Permission-based marketing: the legal and ethical foundation of email (CAN-SPAM, GDPR)
Email vs. other channels: owned audience, no algorithmic gatekeeping, direct relationship
Email marketing maturity: from batch-and-blast to personalized, automated, data-driven programs
lecture_notes.demonstration: Mapping the complete subscriber lifecycle for a SaaS company, identifying the email touchpoints and automation triggers at each stage

review.value: The journey from anonymous visitor to email subscriber to engaged reader to customer to advocate, with specific email strategies appropriate at each stage
The principle that email recipients must explicitly opt in to receive communications; permission builds trust, protects deliverability, and is legally required under CAN-SPAM and GDPR

learning_objectives: Design opt-in strategies that build high-quality subscriber lists
Create segmentation schemes based on demographics, behavior, and engagement
Identify and resolve deliverability issues that prevent emails from reaching inboxes

lecture_notes.introduction: Your email list is your most valuable owned digital asset. The quality of that list, and your ability to segment it for relevant messaging, determines whether your emails land in inboxes and whether subscribers open them.
key_concepts: Opt-in strategies: lead magnets, content upgrades, exit intent, embedded forms, landing pages
Double opt-in vs. single opt-in: confirmation benefits vs. friction trade-offs
Segmentation dimensions: demographics, purchase behavior, engagement level, lifecycle stage, preferences
Deliverability factors: sender reputation, authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), list hygiene, engagement
List hygiene: removing inactive subscribers, handling bounces, sunset policies for unengaged contacts
lecture_notes.demonstration: Setting up a segmented welcome sequence in Mailchimp: creating segments by signup source, designing different welcome paths for each, and configuring deliverability authentication

review.value: A score assigned by email service providers based on sending patterns, complaint rates, bounce rates, and engagement; low reputation routes emails to spam folders regardless of content quality
A valuable resource (ebook, template, checklist, tool) offered in exchange for an email address, functioning as the ethical transaction that begins the subscriber relationship

learning_objectives: Design responsive email templates that render correctly across clients and devices
Write email copy that drives opens (subject lines) and clicks (body copy and CTAs)
Apply accessibility best practices to email design

lecture_notes.introduction: Email design operates under unique constraints: hundreds of email clients render HTML differently, most emails are read on mobile, and the preview pane makes or breaks the open decision. Mastering email-specific design and copywriting is distinct from web or social media skills.
key_concepts: Responsive design: single-column layouts, fluid images, touch-friendly buttons for mobile-first reading
Subject line optimization: personalization, urgency, curiosity, specificity, A/B testing
Preview text: the 40-90 character snippet after the subject line that functions as a second headline
CTA design: single primary CTA, button vs. text link, above-the-fold placement, action-oriented copy
Dark mode compatibility: testing and adapting designs for dark mode email clients
lecture_notes.demonstration: Designing a complete email template in Mailchimp: responsive layout, branded header, scannable body, prominent CTA, and footer with required elements, then testing across email clients using Litmus
setup_requirements: Litmus or Email on Acid free trial for testing

review.value: The snippet displayed after the subject line in the inbox; if not set deliberately, email clients pull the first text in the email body, often 'View in browser' or other non-compelling text that wastes prime real estate
Over 60% of emails are opened on mobile devices; single-column layouts, 44x44px minimum touch targets, and 14-16px body text ensure readability and usability on small screens

learning_objectives: Design automated email sequences triggered by subscriber behavior
Build common automation workflows: welcome series, cart abandonment, re-engagement
Apply personalization and dynamic content based on subscriber data and behavior

lecture_notes.introduction: Marketing automation sends the right message to the right person at the right time without manual intervention. Well-designed automation workflows work 24/7, nurturing subscribers through the lifecycle while you focus on strategy and content creation.
key_concepts: Behavioral triggers: actions (page visit, purchase, form submission) that start automated sequences
Welcome sequence design: introducing the brand, setting expectations, delivering immediate value, first CTA
Cart abandonment recovery: timing, messaging progression, incentive escalation
Re-engagement campaigns: identifying inactive subscribers, win-back messaging, sunset for non-responders
Dynamic content: changing email elements (images, copy, offers) based on subscriber data in real time
lecture_notes.demonstration: Building a complete 5-email welcome sequence in Mailchimp: defining triggers, writing each email, setting delays, adding conditional logic for engagement-based branching, and testing the workflow

review.value: Subscriber actions that automatically start email sequences: signing up, making a purchase, abandoning a cart, visiting a specific page, or becoming inactive; trigger-based emails achieve 3-5x higher open rates than broadcast emails
A series of 3-7 automated emails sent to new subscribers over 1-2 weeks, introducing the brand, delivering promised value, setting communication expectations, and guiding toward a first conversion

learning_objectives: Interpret email marketing metrics to identify performance patterns and optimization opportunities
Design and execute A/B tests for subject lines, content, send times, and CTAs
Calculate email marketing ROI and customer lifetime value from email subscribers

lecture_notes.introduction: Email marketing provides more granular measurement than almost any other channel. Every send, open, click, and conversion is tracked. The challenge is not data availability but data interpretation: knowing which metrics matter, what they mean, and what to do about them.
key_concepts: Core metrics: open rate, click-through rate, click-to-open rate, conversion rate, unsubscribe rate, bounce rate
A/B testing for email: subject line tests, content tests, send time tests, CTA tests
Cohort analysis: comparing performance across subscriber segments and acquisition sources
Revenue attribution: connecting email campaigns to downstream purchases and customer lifetime value
Optimization cycle: test, measure, learn, apply, repeat
lecture_notes.demonstration: Analyzing a month of email campaign data: identifying the best and worst performing emails, diagnosing performance issues, designing three A/B tests, and calculating campaign ROI
setup_requirements: Sample email analytics data (provided on LMS)

review.value: Clicks divided by opens, isolating content effectiveness from subject line effectiveness; if CTOR is low but open rate is high, the subject line works but the email content or CTA needs improvement
Total revenue attributed to email divided by total emails sent, providing a per-send value that can be compared across campaigns and used to project ROI of list growth

questions.question: Explain sender reputation and describe three specific actions that damage it.
questions.answer: Sender reputation is a score assigned by inbox providers (Gmail, Outlook) based on your email sending behavior, determining whether your emails reach the inbox or spam folder. Three actions that damage it: (1) High bounce rates from sending to invalid or outdated email addresses, signaling poor list hygiene. (2) High spam complaint rates from sending to people who did not opt in or who find content irrelevant. (3) Sudden volume spikes, such as sending 50,000 emails when your normal volume is 5,000, which triggers spam filters as potential malicious behavior.

rubric.key: Lists three damaging actions with explanations

questions.question: What is a lead magnet, and why is it more effective than a generic 'subscribe to our newsletter' opt-in for building an email list?
questions.answer: A lead magnet is a specific, valuable resource (ebook, template, checklist, tool, discount) offered in exchange for an email address. It is more effective than a generic newsletter opt-in because: (1) It offers immediate, tangible value rather than a vague promise of future emails. (2) It self-selects for interested subscribers since only people who want that specific resource will sign up. (3) It establishes a value exchange that makes the subscriber feel they received something rather than giving something away. Research shows lead magnet opt-in forms convert 3-5x higher than generic newsletter forms.

questions.question: What is a welcome sequence, and describe the content strategy for a 5-email welcome series for a B2C e-commerce brand.
questions.answer: A welcome sequence is an automated series of emails sent to new subscribers over 1-2 weeks that introduces the brand and guides toward a first purchase. Five-email strategy: (1) Immediate: deliver the promised lead magnet, thank the subscriber, set expectations for email frequency and content. (2) Day 2: share the brand story and values, building emotional connection. (3) Day 4: provide value through educational content related to the subscriber's interest. (4) Day 7: present a first-purchase incentive (discount code, free shipping) with customer testimonials. (5) Day 10: create urgency around the expiring offer with social proof and a clear CTA.

questions.question: Explain the difference between click-through rate and click-to-open rate, and describe a scenario where each metric tells you something different about email performance.
questions.answer: Click-through rate (CTR) divides total clicks by total emails sent, measuring overall campaign effectiveness including deliverability and subject line. Click-to-open rate (CTOR) divides clicks by opens, isolating body content and CTA effectiveness. Scenario: An email has 25% open rate and 2% CTR. CTOR = 2%/25% = 8%. Compared to a second email with 15% open rate and 2.5% CTR, CTOR = 2.5%/15% = 16.7%. The second email's CTR is only slightly higher, but its CTOR is twice as high, revealing that its content and CTA are far more compelling. The first email had a better subject line (higher opens) but weaker content. Optimizing the first email's body content while keeping its subject line could dramatically improve results.

rubric.key: Defines both metrics with correct formulas
Derives actionable insight from the comparison

major_assignments.title: Email Marketing Program Design and Execution
major_assignments.description: Design and build a complete email marketing program for a real or fictional brand, including list building strategy, segmentation plan, automated sequences, campaign templates, and measurement framework. Build working automation sequences in an email platform.
objectives: Design a list building strategy with opt-in mechanisms and lead magnets
Build segmented automated sequences (welcome, nurture, re-engagement)
Create campaign templates for recurring email types
Develop a measurement framework with KPIs and A/B testing plan
deliverables: Email marketing strategy document (8-10 pages) with goals, audience, segmentation, and content plan
Working welcome sequence (3-5 emails) built in Mailchimp or equivalent
Three email campaign templates (newsletter, promotional, announcement) with responsive design
Measurement framework with KPIs, A/B test plan, and reporting schedule
List building recommendations with mockups of opt-in forms and lead magnets

criteria: Strategy aligns email program with business objectives
Segmentation scheme is logical and actionable
Content plan covers the full subscriber lifecycle
Competitive analysis provides useful context
Welcome sequence is properly triggered and timed
Email content follows best practices for each lifecycle stage
Conditional logic demonstrates understanding of behavioral triggers
Sequence is actually built and functional in the email platform
Templates are responsive and render across major clients
Copy follows email-specific best practices
CTAs are clear and strategically placed
Accessibility standards are met
KPIs are defined for each email type and automation
A/B testing plan is specific and prioritized
ROI projection methodology is sound
Reporting cadence and stakeholder communication are planned
Strategic email program aligned with business objectives
Functional automated sequences demonstrating platform proficiency
Professional email design and copywriting
Comprehensive measurement framework with A/B testing plan

quizzes.description: Regular quizzes on email marketing strategy and technical concepts

weekly_exercises.description: Weekly email creation, automation building, and analytics exercises

major_assignment.description: Complete email marketing program design and execution

midterm_examination.description: Practical examination on email strategy, design, and automation

final_examination.description: Cumulative examination including live email program analysis and optimization

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all sessions. Email platform exercises are conducted in class. More than 3 absences result in grade reduction.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late assignments lose 10% per day. Automation projects must be submitted on time for peer testing.
course_policies.collaboration: Platform exercises may involve partner testing. All graded deliverables are individual. Sharing email templates, automation workflows, or strategy documents is prohibited.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All email content must be original. Templates must be built from scratch, not imported from third-party sources. Analytics data must be authentic.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students need a Mailchimp or ConvertKit free account, web browser, and email client access for testing. Litmus free trial is used for cross-client testing.
